titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Jane Davis Doggett Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Adrienne Pruitt

publicationstmt.publisher: Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library Special Collections

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library Special Collections

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid prepared according to Describing Archives: A Content Standard (DACS).

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday December 3, 2025 at 19:00

did.unittitle: Jane Davis Doggett papers

corpname.part: Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library Special Collections

descriptivenote.p: The majority of the materials are in English, with a small amount of material in Spanish or in Russian.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 81, folder 9 (personal papers): Restricted until 2082. For further information contact Arts Library Special Collections.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 107 (audiovisual materials) and box 108 (digital media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Contact Arts Library Special Collections for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This collection is arranged in eleven series: Series I. Artifacts, 1958-2022; Series II. Artwork, circa 1950s-circa 2023; Series III. Biographical Materials, 1940-2023; IV. Civic and Community Activities, 1969-2022; V. Correspondence, 1987-2022; VI. Graphic Design Work, circa 1950s-2022; VII. Personal Papers, 1945-2022; VIII. Professional Papers, circa 1850s-2023; IX. Reference and Source Materials, 1917-2007; X. Audiovisual Materials, circa 1950-circa 2000s; XI. Born-digital Materials, circa 1990s-2023.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Jane Davis Doggett was born in Morristown, Tennessee, on November 4, 1929, to Annie Kate (Weesner) and Robert Doggett, a contractor and horse breeder. She graduated from Hillsboro High School in Nashville in 1948, and received a BFA from Sophie Newcomb College (Tulane) in 1952. After a year of travel and study in Europe, particularly at the Institute of Fine Arts in Florence, Italy, she attended Yale University's School of Art and Architecture. Doggett majored in the relatively new program of graphic design, established in 1951, and received her MFA in 1956. At Yale, she studied with Josef Albers, Alvin Eisenman, Herbert Matter, Paul Rand, and Vincent Scully.

After Yale, Doggett worked at George Nelson Associates, designing directional signage and exhibitions for Colonial Williamsburg, before working as a photographer for Architectural Record. There she covered the Guggenheim Museum in New York as it was under construction, photographed Alvar Aalto and Pier Luigi Nervi and their work, and attended the International Congress of Architects and Engineers of Moscow in 1958. In 1960, Doggett, along with her former Yale classmate Sheila Hicks and photographer Dorothy Jackson, traveled to Mexico to photograph the work of architect Felix Candela for a traveling exhibition. Doggett and Jackson also founded the firm Architectural Graphics Associates (AGA) together.

One of AGA's first projects was the design of the Memphis Airport in 1959. As airports were expanding and growing more complex, Doggett responded with a series of innovations that made wayfinding in vast, unfamiliar surroundings easier on visitors. Using signage that was part of the architecture, relying on simple systems of letter-color-number instead of compass points and company names, and creating "placemarks" that helped people orient themselves with colorful symbols rather than lengthy text, Doggett and AGA pioneered the field that would come to be known as environmental graphics design. Their designs for Memphis Airport received the 1961 American Institute of Architects Award of Merit, the first time the AIA honored architectural graphics design. Doggett would go on to design over 40 international airports, including Baltimore-Washington International Airport, Tampa International Airport, and Houston's George Bush International Airport. She also worked on other large wayfinding and branding projects, notably Madison Square Garden, the Philadelphia Metro transit system, and Vanderbilt Medical Center.

In addition to these projects, Doggett served as an Aesthetics Advisor for the National Advisory Committee on Traffic Control Devices in the Department of Transportation (1965-1975) and was an adjunct professor at the University of Bridgeport, where she taught an architectural graphics design course (1971-1974). In 1976, AGA set up a satellite office in Fort Lauderdale, Florida, and in 1988 Doggett designed her residence on Jupiter Island, Florida.

Doggett's creativity was also expressed in her artwork. Beginning in 2000, she embarked on a series she dubbed "IconoChromes" or "Talking Graphics," in which philosophical messages were conveyed via simple, colorful graphic images. This resulted in museum exhibitions, a series of books, and related merchandise. Her sculptural work, "23rd Psalm," was acquired by the Yale University Art Gallery in 2012, and is on permanent loan to the Saint Thomas More Catholic Chapel and Center. She was inducted into the Florida Artists Hall of Fame in 2016, and in 2018 received the Society for Experiential Graphic Design Fellow award. The same year, a documentary film about her life and career, Jane Davis Doggett: Wayfinder in the Jet Age, was released, and aired on PBS in 2019.
Doggett died on April 10, 2023, in Sun City Center, Florida.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Jane Davis Doggett papers, Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The Jane Davis Doggett papers document her work as a graphic designer and artist, known for her pioneering work in wayfinding for large urban environments such as airports. Materials include correspondence, contracts and proposals; project files; original artwork; photographs, slides, and audiovisual materials; exhibition files; records related to her books, including Talking Graphics (2007); and biographical materials. There is also a small amount of realia, including models.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright was retained by Jane Davis Doggett for materials she authored or otherwise produced. After the lifetime of Jane Davis Doggett, copyright passed to Yale University. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user. Requests for publication of materials should be directed to Doggett's literary advisor, Basie Gitlin.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Organization of this collection adheres as closely as feasible to the received order. Wherever possible, original folder titles have been retained, with the addition of format information and dates. Duplicates and personal financial information was deaccessioned.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Related material: Art, architecture, and art history theses and projects, Yale University (RU 259)

did.unittitle: Artifacts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Artifacts include awards, mockups, models, samples, a seal, signage and framed items, and objects of inspiration used in Doggett's creative practice.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Artifacts are arranged by type and size.

did.unittitle: Paper mockups of 3-D IconoChrome sculptures

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Seven paper mockups in various shapes (rectangle, triangle, arch, square, pentagon).

did.unittitle: Tampa International Airport sign
Invitation to International Union of Architects Reception

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Framed invitation to Jane Doggett for a closing reception concluding the fifth congress of the International Union of Architects, sponsored by the USSR Architects' Union and taking place on July 26 1958 at the Grand Kremlin Palace.

did.unittitle: Tampa International Airport model airplane
Artwork

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes artworks from all stages of Doggett's life, including student photography and drawings from her time at Newcomb College and Yale University, the Talking Graphics / IconoChromes series that she created digitally beginning in the 2000s, and the sketches, portraits, and reference images compiled over seven decades.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Artwork is arranged in three subseries: Drawings and reference images; Student work; and Talking Graphics and Iconochromes.

Talking Graphics and IconoChromes

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This subseries is arranged chronologically, and then alphabetically.

Biographical Materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes drafts of Doggett's memoir, the images she intended to use in its illustration, and the source materials she gathered while writing it, as well as publications by and about Doggett.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged in two subseries: Memoir and Publications.

did.unittitle: Memoir

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Note that the date assigned to Memoir materials generally refers to the date of compilation. Many materials in this subseries are undated, are copies, and were originally created at various points in Doggett's life, but were compiled from 2019-2023. Where the date of creation could be discerned for original materials, that date has been used.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Memoir records are arranged in 3 groupings: Drafts, Images, and Source Materials, to preserve the original order as much as possible.

did.unittitle: Drafts

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Drafts are arranged roughly by order of composition and maintain received order.

title.part: Tulane School of Liberal Arts Magazine

did.unittitle: Bibliography
Jane Davis Doggett memorial program
Civic and Community Activities

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains materials documenting Doggett's civic and community activities, including her service as the president of the Arts Foundation for Martin County (1984-1986); chairman of the committee (1992-1996) and vice-president (2000-2002) of the Jupiter Island Garden Club; Jupiter Island Town Commissioner (1995-1999); and as a member of the advisory committee for The Nature Conservancy in the 2000s. Also documented is her volunteer work for the Winter Harbor Yacht Club, for the Jupiter Island art community, and for Girls Achieving Leadership in Aviation.

Correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes both personal and business correspondence, notably with George Bush and his family, and with Sandra Day O'Connor.

did.unittitle: Bush family correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes fifty-first presidential inauguration materials.

did.unittitle: Sandra Day O'Connor correspondence
Google lawsuit correspondence
Correspondence
Ideas to pursue - correspondence and clippings

JDD to file correspondence

Ralph Woodward
Graphic Design Work

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series encompasses the wayfinding projects at airports and other large public spaces completed by Doggett and her staff; the alphabets they created for maximum legibility at a distance; and other branding projects. It includes general business records, design manuals and programs, and source materials, in addition to project plans, sketches, design records, and photographs and slides documenting the work.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged in five subseries: Airports and other wayfinding projects; Alphabets; General business records; Miscellaneous graphic design; and Slides.

did.unittitle: Airports and other wayfinding projects

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This subseries is arranged alphabetically by project name.

General business records

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Doggett founded Architectural Graphics Associates with Dorothy Jackson. It was incorporated in Connecticut in 1962, and in 1980, Doggett bought out Jackson's shares in the business. In 1993, the business became Doggett's consultancy, Jane Davis Doggett, Inc. In the late 1990s, Doggett explored the sale of her business to other companies, and prepared potential acquisition binders and a series of brochures showcasing her portfolio to support this endeavor, as well as to present to potential clients.

Personal Papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes Christmas cards designed by Doggett, her calendars, art collection records, and photographs and stereographs documenting her travels. There are also yearbooks from Hillsboro High School and Newcomb College, and records relating to her properties.

Beach photographs

scopecontent.p: This file contains both personal Christmas cards and those created for Doggett's businesses. There are a small number of other holiday mailers.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted until 2082. For further information contact Arts Library Special Collections.

Professional Papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains records of Doggerr's professional activities, including information about awards that Doggett received; records pertaining to conferences and lectures that she attended and at which she spoke; exhibition documentation; and the photography work she did for Architectural Record and as a freelancer in the 1950s and 1960s.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged in four subseries: Awards; Conferences and lectures; Exhibitions; and Photography.

Reference and Source Materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes general reference books and type specimens, color swatches and samples, and a large collection of books on proverbs, which inspired many of Doggett's Talking Graphics and IconoChrome images.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged in three subseries: Color; General reference books; and Proverbs.

General reference books

Proverbs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Doggett's book on proverbs often include sketches related to the text, which have been preserved in situ. There is also one book (Architecture, Reredos, Stained Glass, Bells, Needlepoint, 1961) designed by Doggett and her business partner Dorothy Jackson, which is maintained in this subseries to preserve received order.

did.unittitle: 1000 Kikuyu Proverbs , by G. Barra

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1939, reprint 1998.

did.unittitle: ABC Dictionary of Chinese Proverbs , by John S. Rohsenow
African Proverbs and Wisdom , by Julia Stewart
Ancient Egyptian Proverbs , by Muata Ashby
Apricots Tomorrow , by Primrose Arnander and Ashkhain Skipwith
Arabic Proverbs , by Joseph Hanki
Architecture, Reredos, Stained Glass, Bells, Needlepoint , St. Mark's Church, New Canaan, Connecticut, designed by Jane Doggett and Dorothy Jackson
As They Say in Zanzibar , by David Crystal
The A-Z of African Proverbs , by Jan Knappert
Burmese Proverbs , by Hla Pe
California-Spanish Proverbs , by Mildred Yorba Macarthur
Collected Poems , by Robert Frost
The Conformers , by Jack Wohl
Confucius Said , by J. L. Lu
Danish Proverbs , by Julie Jensen McDonald and Esther Feske
Dictionary of 1000 German Proverbs , by Peter Mertvago
A Dictionary of American Proverbs and Proverbial Phrases , by Archer Taylor and Bartlett Jere Whiting
A Dictionary of English and Italian Equivalent Proverbs , by Teodor Flonta
A Dictionary of English and Portuguese Equivalent Proverb s, by Teodor Flonta
A Dictionary of Hindustani Proverbs , by S.W. Fallon
A Dictionary of Kashmiri Proverbs and Sayings , by J. H. Knowles
A Dictionary of Mexican American Proverbs , by Mark Glazer
Divers Proverbs , by Nathan Bailey
Drumbeats of Black Africa, A Collection of African Proverbs , by O. Charles Okwelume, Jnr
Dutch Proverbs , by Holly Flame Heusinkveld and Jean Caris-Osland
Even Monkeys Fall From Trees , by David Galef
Finnish Proverbs , by Inkeri Vaananen-Jensen
Folk Wisdom of Mexico , by Jeff M. Sellers
Fountain of Japanese Proverbs , by Taiji Takashima
Greek Proverbs , by D.S. Baker
Haud Yer Wheesht!' Your Scottish Granny's Favourite Sayings , by Allan Morrison
The House of the Heart Is Never Full and Other Proverbs of Africa , by Guy T. Zona
If You Have Two Loaves of Bread, Sell One and Buy a Lily and Other Proverbs of China , by Guy. A Zona
Irish Proverbs , by Karen Bailey
Japanese Proverbs and Sayings , by Daniel Crump Buchanan
The look again... and again, and again, and again book. , by Beau Gardner
Malay Proverbs , by Sir Richard Winstedt
Mother Goose Rhymes , by Robert A. Propper
Persian Proverbs , by L. P. Elwell-Sutton
The Pilgrims Proverbs of Nepal and Other Countries , by Kesar Lall
Polish Proverbs , by Joanne Asala
A Proverb for It , by B.J. Marketos
Proverbs and Common Sayings from the Chinese , by Arthur H. Smith
Putnam's Complete Book of Quotations , by W. Gurney Benham
Treasury of African Love : Poems & Proverbs in the languages of Africa & English , by Nicholas Awde
Treasury of Hungarian Love : Poems, Quotations & Proverbs in Hungarian and English , by Katherine Gyekenyesi Gatto
Treasury of Proverbs and Epigrams , Avenel Books
Treasury of Roman Love : Poems, Quotations & Proverbs in Latin and English , by Richard A. Branyon
The Seventeen Traditions , by Ralph Nader
The Son of a Duck is a Floater and other Arab sayings with English equivalents , by Primrose Arnander and Ashkhain Skipwith
Unload Your Own Donkey : Arabic sayings from Egypt and Other Countries , by Primrose Arnander and Ashkhain Skipwith

The Wit and Wisdom of the Talmud , by George Lankevich
Words of Wisdom from the Vikings , by Joanne Asala
Zulu Proverbs , by C.L.S. Nyembezi
Audiovisual Materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists mainly of Super 8 movies documenting Doggett's travels in Europe in the 1950s; there are also reels related to airport topics. In addition, an audiocassette contains music from the Yale University Guild of Carillonneurs, and there is a videotape of a farewell party.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged alphabetically by material type, and then by title.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Contact Arts Library Special Collections for further information.

Born-digital Materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of Doggett's external hard drives, CDs, and DVDs.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Contact Arts Library Special Collections for further information.
